seniority.level: Senior Manager (IC or people manager unclear)
level_signals: 10+ years in a similar role in RevOps or sales operations
Senior Manager title reporting to Head of Revenue Operations
Owns architecture of GTM processes, systems, and data

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years in a similar role in RevOps or sales operations, preferably in AI, SaaS, or enterprise software.
must_haves.qualifier: preferably in AI, SaaS, or enterprise software

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience designing and scaling GTM processes and systems across multiple functions.
Strong understanding of data governance principles in a GTM context: data quality, taxonomy, ownership, and lifecycle management.
Deep, hands-on experience with Salesforce and a strong instinct for when to configure, integrate, or cut a tool.
Proven ability to translate business goals into structured, executable operational plans across multiple GTM functions.
End-to-end understanding of B2B SaaS revenue mechanics: lead-to-opportunity, quote-to-cash, renewal management, and ARR recognition.
A clear communicator who can move between technical and non-technical stakeholders without losing either audience, and who operates with high ownership and low ego.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years in a similar role in RevOps or sales operations, preferably in AI, SaaS, or enterprise software.

scope.surface: GTM Systems — Revenue Operations covering Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success
primary_metrics_inferred: Revenue target attainment across Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success
core_responsibilities: Partner with GTM leadership to define go-to-market strategies, build reliable forecasts, and establish the performance metrics that keep Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success accountable to shared revenue targets.
Streamline and document GTM processes with growth in mind, building systems that reduce friction today and scale with the business as it grows.
Evaluate, scope, and optimize the GTM tech stack to support scale.
Own the integrity of GTM data, defining standards for data entry, structure, and ownership across the revenue stack.
Translate complex operational findings into clear narratives for executive audiences, and ensure GTM leadership and the C-suite are always working from the same picture.

green_flags: Clear reporting structure stated (reports to Head of Revenue Operations)
Specific, well-scoped responsibilities with no vague filler
Encourages applicants who don't perfectly match all requirements
Strong benefits package: 6 weeks vacation, 100% parental leave top-up, full health/dental
Remote-flexible policy with co-working stipend — not strictly on-site despite Toronto listing
Mission-driven company in a high-growth AI space
red_flags: Location listed as Toronto only, but 'remote-flexible' mentioned in perks — ambiguity about whether fully remote is permitted for this role
Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements not stated
Whether the role manages a team is not specified despite 'Senior Manager' title

'preferably in AI, SaaS, or enterprise software' for the 10+ years requirement is listed in must_haves but also captured as nice_to_have due to the 'preferably' qualifier — the base years requirement is hard

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.manages_people (not stated; defaulted to false as no direct reports mentioned)

ambiguities: Location field says 'Toronto' but perks section states 'Remote-flexible, offices in Toronto, New York, San Francisco, London and Paris, as well as a co-working stipend' — unclear if this role is remote-eligible or Toronto-only. Treated as hybrid with Toronto as primary location.

The '10+ years preferably in AI, SaaS, or enterprise software' conflates a hard requirement (years) with a soft preference (industry). Captured as a hard must-have for the years component and a soft nice-to-have for the industry qualifier.
